entries.title: Shoto Leads The Kamino Dabi Matchup

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shoto is assigned to confront Dabi because the battle is both tactical and family-specific.
content.injection: Center Shoto’s role as both hero and brother; the fight is not merely a firepower contest.

content.fact: Shoto’s Phosphor technique uses both sides of his Quirk to stabilize and counter extreme heat.
content.injection: Use Phosphor as a hard-earned integration of Shoto’s dual inheritance, not an instant generic ice blast.

entries.title: Dabi First Defeat Is Not The End

context.label: Shoto defeats Dabi at Kamino first time

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Shoto appears to stop Dabi at Kamino, but Dabi’s body and hatred continue driving the conflict later.
content.injection: Do not treat the Kamino victory as the complete Todoroki resolution; it delays a deeper family crisis.

entries.title: Dabi’s Body Is Failing Under His Own Flames

context.label: Shoto defeats Dabi at Kamino first time

content.fact: Dabi’s power is inseparable from bodily collapse; his hatred pushes him through damage that should stop him.
content.injection: Portray Dabi’s threat as self-destructive and horrific, not sustainable fire mastery.

content.fact: The Todoroki family converges to stop Toya because Endeavor alone cannot repair the harm or contain the crisis.
content.injection: Make the family intervention collective; this is not a clean Endeavor redemption duel.

content.injection: Use Iida’s role as support and relay; his contribution is crucial even when he is not the emotional center.

entries.title: Endeavor Cannot Undo Toya With One Speech

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Endeavor’s guilt and recognition do not erase Toya’s suffering or the family’s damage.
content.injection: Avoid sudden reconciliation; this confrontation should remain painful, incomplete, and consequence-heavy.

entries.title: Rei Facing Toya Is Necessary Not Decorative

content.fact: Rei’s presence matters because Toya’s tragedy involves the entire family system, not just Endeavor.

entries.title: Dabi Copies Movement More Than Balance

context.label: Shoto defeats Dabi at Kamino first time

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Dabi can imitate or adapt from Shoto’s movement, but he lacks Shoto’s balanced fire-and-ice stability.
content.injection: Do not give Dabi true safe Phosphor mastery; his imitation is dangerous and unstable.

entries.title: Endeavor Chooses To Burn With Toya If Needed

content.fact: Endeavor is prepared to die containing Toya’s explosion before the rest of the family intervenes.
content.injection: Use Endeavor’s intent as despairing accountability, not a triumphant sacrifice.
